_meta.author: refine-scorecard / research assistant
_meta.note: Evidence-only finalization of Richard Hayes (TX HD-57). Replaces party-default archetype (all-true inferred) with cited evidence. Corrects bio: NOT a 2024 freshman -- first took office Jan 10 2023 (won 2022 in new HD-57, reelected 2024). Catholic. Attorney. Freedom Caucus. Candidacy currency updated: won 2026 R primary (Mar 3 2026), general vs Ray Stith (D) Nov 3 2026.

set.office: Texas State Representative (HD-57, sitting; 2026 general candidate)

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (member, Immaculate Conception Catholic Church, Denton)
profile.background: Attorney; founding partner of Hayes, Berry, White & Vanzant LLP (Denton); board-certified in commercial and residential real estate law. BBA Texas Christian Univ., MBA Texas A&M, JD St. Mary's. Former Assistant Criminal DA, Bexar County; former federal briefing attorney. Parliamentarian of the Republican Party of Texas. First took office Jan 10, 2023 (HD-57 created in 2021 redistricting); reelected 2024. Texas House Freedom Caucus member.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-19 — Evidence-based finalization (web-verified). TLO 89th-Lege member page confirms current HD-57 seat + committees. Texas Right to Life 89th score 100; Texas Alliance for Life 89th 93%; YCT 88th 98 (2nd most conservative in House). SB 2 (school choice/ESA) voted YES. Self-stated 2A (TSRA + NRA + GOA Life Member) and Catholic faith from campaign bio. Candidacy: won 2026 R primary, general vs Ray Stith (D). Bio corrected: not a 2024 freshman — in office since Jan 2023.

richard-hayes.notes_set: Republican, HD-57 (Denton County: Lake Dallas/Hickory Creek area). Attorney; Catholic; Texas House Freedom Caucus. EVIDENCE: Texas Right to Life 89th-Lege score 100 (texasrighttolife.com/legislative-scores) and Texas Alliance for Life 89th score 93% (HARD) anchor strong pro-life record. Voted YES on SB 2 universal school choice/ESA, 2025 (only R no-votes were Phelan & VanDeaver) — HARD. YCT 88th-Lege score 98, 2nd most conservative House member (HARD). Self-states: 'strong Second Amendment supporter,' Life Member of TSRA, NRA, and Gun Owners of America (campaign bio, HARD self-statement). Campaign 'issues': border security, property-tax relief, election integrity, school choice, deregulation (self-stated, vague on some specifics). NO documented evidence found on: same-sex marriage votes, transgender/LGBTQ legislation votes, IVF/embryo, euthanasia, CBDC, paper ballots, red-flag specifics, 10th-Amendment nullification — left null (not inferred). No formal TRTL/NRA-PVF/Defend Texas Liberty campaign endorsement records located. Candidacy currency: won 2026 R primary (Mar 3 2026); general vs Ray Stith (D) Nov 3 2026.

0.note: Texas Right to Life 89th-Lege score 100; Texas Alliance for Life 89th score 93% (scored bills incl. SB 31 Life of the Mother Act, SB 33 ban on taxpayer-funded abortion travel, HB 7 mail-order abortion-drug enforcement). HARD — recorded-vote ratings.

1.note: Scored pro-life record is restriction/enforcement bills, not abortion-abolition. No evidence he voted for abolition specifically. Null.
